Worksheet Operations

Complete documentation for worksheet management in Aspose.Cells FOSS for .NET

â†?Back to Index

Worksheet Overview

The Worksheet class represents a single sheet in an Excel workbook and provides comprehensive functionality for managing cells, data, formatting, and worksheet-level properties.

Key Features:
  • Access and modify worksheet properties (name, visibility, tab color, etc.)
  • Manage cells and cell data through the Cells collection
  • Handle formulas and calculations
  • Apply cell formatting and styles
  • Work with merged cells
  • Manage hyperlinks, data validation, and conditional formatting
  • Configure page setup and print settings
  • Protect and unprotect worksheets

Accessing Worksheets

By Index
// Access worksheet by zero-based index
var firstWorksheet = workbook.Worksheets[0];
var secondWorksheet = workbook.Worksheets[1];
By Name
// Access worksheet by name
var sheet = workbook.Worksheets["Sheet1"];
Iterating Worksheets
// Iterate through all worksheets
foreach (var worksheet in workbook.Worksheets)
{
    Console.WriteLine($"Worksheet: {worksheet.Name}");
}
Creating New Worksheets
// Add a new worksheet
int newSheetIndex = workbook.Worksheets.Add();`r`nvar newSheet = workbook.Worksheets[newSheetIndex];

// Add and name in one operation
int summarySheetIndex = workbook.Worksheets.Add("Summary");`r`nvar summarySheet = workbook.Worksheets[summarySheetIndex];

// The new sheet is automatically positioned at the end
Removing Worksheets
// Remove by index
workbook.Worksheets.RemoveAt(0);

// Remove by name
workbook.Worksheets.RemoveAt("Sheet2");

// Remove worksheet object
var sheet = workbook.Worksheets[0];
workbook.Worksheets.RemoveAt(sheet.Name);

Worksheet Properties

Name Management
// Get worksheet name
string name = worksheet.Name;

// Set worksheet name
worksheet.Name = "Financial Data";

// Name validation:
// - Must be unique within workbook
// - Cannot be empty
// - Cannot contain special characters: \ / ? * [ ] :
Visibility Control
// Set worksheet visibility
worksheet.VisibilityType = VisibilityType.Visible;      // Normal sheet
worksheet.VisibilityType = VisibilityType.Hidden;        // Hidden but accessible by name
worksheet.VisibilityType = VisibilityType.VeryHidden;    // Completely hidden

// Get current visibility
VisibilityType visibility = worksheet.VisibilityType;
Tab Color
// Set tab color
worksheet.TabColor = System.Drawing.Color.Red;
worksheet.TabColor = System.Drawing.Color.FromArgb(255, 128, 0);

// Remove tab color
worksheet.TabColor = System.Drawing.Color.Empty;
Display Options
// Show/hide gridlines
worksheet.ShowGridlines = false;
worksheet.ShowGridlines = true;

// Show/hide row and column headers
worksheet.ShowRowColumnHeaders = false;

// Set zoom level (percentage)
worksheet.Zoom = 150;  // 150% zoom
worksheet.Zoom = 75;   // 75% zoom
Worksheet Position
// Move worksheet to specific position
workbook.Worksheets[0].MoveTo(2);  // Move to index 2

// Get worksheet index
int index = worksheet.Index;

Cell Operations

Accessing Cells
// Access cells collection
var cells = worksheet.Cells;

// By cell name (A1 notation)
var cellA1 = cells["A1"];
var cellB5 = cells["B5"];

// By row and column (zero-based)
var cell0_0 = cells[0, 0];     // A1
var cell4_1 = cells[4, 1];     // B5

// Get cell by name directly
var cell = worksheet.Cells["A1"];
Setting Cell Values
// String value
cells["A1"].PutValue("Hello World");

// Numeric values
cells["A2"].PutValue(42);
cells["A3"].PutValue(3.14159);

// Boolean
cells["A4"].PutValue(true);
cells["A5"].PutValue(false);

// DateTime
cells["A6"].PutValue(DateTime.Now);
cells["A7"].PutValue(new DateTime(2026, 5, 13));
Getting Cell Values
// Get logical value
object value = cell.Value;

// Get string representation
string stringValue = cell.StringValue;

// Get display string with formatting
string displayString = cell.DisplayStringValue;

// Check cell type
CellType type = cell.Type;  // IsString, IsNumber, IsBoolean, IsDateTime, etc.
Batch Cell Operations
// Get used data range
int maxRow = cells.MaxDataRow;
int maxCol = cells.MaxDataColumn;

// Iterate through used range
for (int row = 0; row <= maxRow; row++)
{
    for (int col = 0; col <= maxCol; col++)
    {
        var cell = cells[row, col];
        if (cell.Value != null)
        {
            Console.WriteLine($"{cell.Name}: {cell.StringValue}");
        }
    }
}

Merged Cells

Merging Cells
// Merge a range of cells
worksheet.Cells.Merge(0, 0, 1, 3);  // Merge A1:C1
// Parameters: firstRow, firstColumn, totalRows, totalColumns

// Merge for title
cells["A1"].PutValue("Quarterly Report");
worksheet.Cells.Merge(0, 0, 1, 5);  // A1:E1

// Merge for subtotal
cells["A10"].PutValue("Total:");
worksheet.Cells.Merge(9, 0, 1, 2);  // A10:B10
Unmerging Cells
// Unmerge a specific range
worksheet.Cells.UnMerge(0, 0, 1, 3);  // Unmerge A1:C1

// Unmerge all merges in worksheet
worksheet.Cells.UnMerge();
Checking Merged Cells
// Check if cell is merged
if (cell.IsMerged)
{
    Console.WriteLine("Cell is part of a merged range");
}

// Get merged range
var mergedRanges = worksheet.Cells.MergedCells;
foreach (var range in mergedRanges)
{
    Console.WriteLine($"Merged range: {range}");
}

Worksheet Protection

Protecting Worksheets
// Protect worksheet (default protection)
worksheet.Protect();

// Protect with password
worksheet.Protect("myPassword");

// Protect with specific options
worksheet.Protect(
    password: "secure123",
    allowFormattingCells: true,
    allowFormattingColumns: false,
    allowFormattingRows: false,
    allowInsertingColumns: false,
    allowDeletingColumns: false
);
Unprotecting Worksheets
// Unprotect (no password)
worksheet.Unprotect();

// Unprotect with password
worksheet.Unprotect("myPassword");

// Unprotect regardless of password (use with caution)
worksheet.Unprotect(string.Empty);
Checking Protection Status
// Check if worksheet is protected
bool isProtected = worksheet.IsProtected;
bool isPasswordProtected = worksheet.IsPasswordProtected;

if (isProtected)
{
    Console.WriteLine("Worksheet is protected");
}

Complete Example

Create and Populate Worksheet
using Aspose.Cells_FOSS;
using System;

class Program
{
    static void Main()
    {
        var workbook = new Workbook();

        // Create new worksheet
        int worksheetIndex = workbook.Worksheets.Add("Sales Data");`r`n        var worksheet = workbook.Worksheets[worksheetIndex];

        // Set worksheet properties
        worksheet.TabColor = System.Drawing.Color.Blue;
        worksheet.Zoom = 110;

        // Create header row
        cells = worksheet.Cells;
        cells["A1"].PutValue("Product");
        cells["B1"].PutValue("Quantity");
        cells["C1"].PutValue("Price");
        cells["D1"].PutValue("Total");

        // Merge header cells
        worksheet.Cells.Merge(0, 0, 1, 4);

        // Apply formatting to header
        var headerStyle = cells["A1"].GetStyle();
        headerStyle.Font.Bold = true;
        headerStyle.Font.Color = System.Drawing.Color.White;
        headerStyle.BackgroundColor = System.Drawing.Color.FromArgb(0, 51, 153);
        cells["A1"].SetStyle(headerStyle);

        // Add data rows
        string[] products = { "Widget A", "Widget B", "Widget C" };
        int[] quantities = { 100, 150, 200 };
        double[] prices = { 10.50, 15.75, 8.99 };

        for (int i = 0; i < products.Length; i++)
        {
            int row = i + 2;
            cells[row, 0].PutValue(products[i]);
            cells[row, 1].PutValue(quantities[i]);
            cells[row, 2].PutValue(prices[i]);

            // Calculate total
            cells[row, 3].Formula = $"=B{row}*C{row}";
        }

        // Add grand total
        int lastRow = products.Length + 3;
        cells[lastRow, 0].PutValue("Grand Total:");
        cells[lastRow, 3].Formula = "=SUM(D2:D" + (lastRow - 1) + ")";

        // Format total row
        var totalStyle = cells[lastRow, 0].GetStyle();
        totalStyle.Font.Bold = true;
        cells[lastRow, 0].SetStyle(totalStyle);
        cells[lastRow, 3].SetStyle(totalStyle);

        // Save workbook
        workbook.Save("sales-report.xlsx");
        Console.WriteLine("Worksheet created successfully!");
    }
}
Process Multiple Worksheets
using Aspose.Cells_FOSS;
using System;

class Program
{
    static void Main()
    {
        var workbook = new Workbook("multi-sheet-workbook.xlsx");

        // Process each worksheet
        foreach (var worksheet in workbook.Worksheets)
        {
            Console.WriteLine($"Processing: {worksheet.Name}");

            // Skip hidden worksheets
            if (worksheet.VisibilityType == VisibilityType.VeryHidden)
            {
                Console.WriteLine("  Skipped (very hidden)");
                continue;
            }

            // Get cells collection
            var cells = worksheet.Cells;

            // Check if worksheet has data
            if (cells.MaxDataRow >= 0 && cells.MaxDataColumn >= 0)
            {
                Console.WriteLine($"  Data range: A1:{cells.GetCellByIndex(cells.MaxDataRow, cells.MaxDataColumn).Name}");

                // Count non-empty cells
                int nonEmptyCount = 0;
                for (int row = 0; row <= cells.MaxDataRow; row++)
                {
                    for (int col = 0; col <= cells.MaxDataColumn; col++)
                    {
                        if (cells[row, col].Value != null)
                        {
                            nonEmptyCount++;
                        }
                    }
                }
                Console.WriteLine($"  Non-empty cells: {nonEmptyCount}");
            }
            else
            {
                Console.WriteLine("  No data found");
            }

            // Update worksheet name if needed
            if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(worksheet.Name))
            {
                worksheet.Name = $"Sheet_{worksheet.Index + 1}";
            }
        }

        // Save processed workbook
        workbook.Save("processed-workbook.xlsx");
        Console.WriteLine("Processing complete!");
    }
}

Important Notes

  • Indexing: Worksheet and cell indexing is zero-based.
  • Cell Names: Use A1 notation (e.g., "A1", "B5") for cell references in names and formulas.
  • Merged Cells: Only the top-left cell in a merged range can have a value.
  • Protection: Protecting a worksheet does not protect individual cells; cells must be locked individually.
  • Visibility: VeryHidden worksheets cannot be accessed through the Excel interface, only programmatically.
  • Performance: Batch operations are more efficient than individual cell operations when working with large datasets.
